\boxed{A}
Clinical reasoning: While the initial symptoms of both COVID-19 and influenza are nonspecific and overlap broadly, certain clinical features—such as loss of taste or smell in COVID-19—are distinctive enough to aid differentiation when recognized. Additionally, advances in diagnostic testing allow for precise distinction via molecular assays targeting specific pathogens. Although subtle, these identifiable markers support the ability to clinically distinguish them under appropriate circumstances.
